1.6 We also recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, strictly
observe the pertinent procedures relating to adjustments for stale checks and
valid reconciling items, as prescribed by Section 59 of the NGAS-LGUs
Manual and COA Circular No. 96-011, respectively.

2.6 We recommended and Management agreed to observe the 30-day period of
liquidation of cash advances for official travels by refunding the excess
balances upon submission of liquidation reports, and enforce the immediate
return of cash advances for cancelled/ unattended travels.

3.8 We recommended and Management agreed to:
3.8.a Through the Municipal Accountant, take up the following proposed
adjusting journal entries to arrive at an accurate balance of the CIP
account in the ensuing FS:

Accounts Payable or other 12,376,226.45

₱14,503,649.10 were already reclassified to the appropriate asset account
upon completion as of May 2025.

3.8.b Ensure that the Accounting and Engineering Departments perform
periodic reconciliation between the CIP ledger account and project
records; and

3.8.c Record only the costs of ongoing infrastructure projects in the CIP
accounts, ensuring that amounts are supported by the SWA or
progress billing, as verified by the project engineer.

4.12 We recommended that Management:
4.12.a Submit a duly signed DV No. 2024-12-79 or certification that the LCE
approved the payment in said DV;
4.12.b Ensure that proposed project(s) is/are readily implementable to
prevent delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects;
4.12.c Ensure that all documents are properly signed before payments are
made; and
4.12.d Observe strict compliance with the guidelines in the implementation
of fund transfers.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer, henceforth, ensure actual cash transfer of the unexpended
LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE to the TF books of accounts at yearend.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed to:
6.7.a Identify the PPAs to be funded from the prior years’ STF separate
from those to be charged to savings from capital outlay;
6.7.b Revert to the TF any unexpended balance of the prior years’ (CYs
2021-2023) STF transferred to GF;
6.7.c Refrain from transferring unutilized DRRMF from TF to GF without the
lapse of five years; and
6.7.d Henceforth, comply strictly with the accounting and reporting
guidelines for the LDRRMF provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

12.3 We recommended and Management agreed to continue the enforcement on
the settlement of audit disallowances.

remittance/submission of
their collections be done at
least once a week, or as
soon as the collections
reach ₱5,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the fund transfer of intra-agency funds to their appropriate bank accounts; and henceforth, immediately fund transfer the share of special accounts from the income of the Municipality, and strictly monitor the preparation of check payments to avoid erroneous charging of disbursements to other accounts and the eventual bloating of the intra-agency accounts; and
Henceforth, ensure that the Municipal Accountant eliminate the Intra-Agency accounts in the Consolidated FS at yearend.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the Road Networks account with the following entry to correct the ensuing FS:

Henceforth, ensure that no residual value shall be provided for the depreciable components of the road networks account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the following adjusting journal entries for the stale checks and identified book reconciling items to arrive at an accurate balance of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts in the ensuing FS:

Henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent procedures relating to adjustments for stale checks and valid reconciling items, as prescribed in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government and COA Circular No. 96-011, respectively.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize RPT using the modified accrual basis and the other income using the cash basis on the year when income was collected and not on the year it was submitted to ensure fair presentation of income in the financial statements. Likewise, the MT agreed to submit RCDs promptly especially collections and remittances at yearend for the proper accounting thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Ensure that all RCOs submit their collections daily and intact by closely supervising and monitoring the remittances of their collections through strict enforcement of Section 29 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and recommend to the LCE for the imposition of appropriate penal sanctions to revenue collectors who repeatedly fail to remit on time, if necessary;
Consider not issuing additional accountable forms to the RCOs unless collections pertaining to the accountable forms previously issued to them are remitted intact or an inventory of any unused accountable form had been diligently prepared; and
Ensure that for field collectors, where collections are minimal and daily remittance or deposit is costly and impractical, remittance/submission of their collections be done at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that:
The Property and Supply Officer conduct periodic physical count of inventories semestrally and prepare the RPCI to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Property and Supply Officer reconcile their inventory records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the actual transfer of the correct amount of unexpended QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE from the GF to the STF books of accounts with the following accounting journal entries:
General Fund Books:
Trust Fund Books:

Henceforth, observe the proper classification and correctness of the LDRRMF balances in order to provide an accurate basis for the transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE to the TF books of accounts at yearend.

We recommended and the MDRRM Council, through the MDRRM Officer, agreed to review the needs of the Municipality on DRRM and optimize the use of the STF; further, to update the Municipality’s current LDRRMFIP to include PPAs to be funded therefrom.
We reiterated our recommendation that Management continue its efforts to collect the unsettled disallowances.

observations.managementAction: No action.

observations.recommendation:
1.6	We have recommended and management agreed to require the accounting and treasury offices to review the unrecorded check disbursements to ascertain the validity of the expenditures and to ensure the submission of the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents for proper recording in the books.
Also, we have recommended and management agreed to coordinate with the depository bank for proper identification of the unrecorded deposits for proper recording in the books of the LGU.

2.5 	We have recommended that management:
require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees concerned on the existence of these checks that had become stale and issue a replacement check only upon submission of the stale check.
require the Municipal Accountant to draw the following proposed adjusting journal entry to cancel the stale check and recognize the outstanding payable, pending the replacement of the check:

Cash In Bank – LCCA | 1-07-03-010 | ₱23,352.16 | 
Accounts Payable | 3-01-01-010 |  | ₱23,352.16
To cancel the stale check and recognize corresponding accounts payable. | To cancel the stale check and recognize corresponding accounts payable. | To cancel the stale check and recognize corresponding accounts payable. | To cancel the stale check and recognize corresponding accounts payable.

3.10 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
The Municipal Accountant to draw the appropriate adjusting entry to correct the errors noted as follows:
The Property Unit to cancel the Property Acknowledgment Receipt, if any, and issue the Inventory Custodian Slip for the small tangible items; and
The Municipal Accountant to adjust the PPE accounts by dropping the costs of the small tangible items noted and their corresponding accumulated depreciation amounting to ₱6,817,885.30 and ₱4,662,590.92, respectively.

Accounts Payable | 2-01-01-010 | ₱247,869.31 | 
Hospitals and Health Center | 1-07-04-030 |  | ₱247,869.31
To adjust the erroneous recording of the asset for completed project (Improvement of RHU Phase II) | To adjust the erroneous recording of the asset for completed project (Improvement of RHU Phase II) | To adjust the erroneous recording of the asset for completed project (Improvement of RHU Phase II) | To adjust the erroneous recording of the asset for completed project (Improvement of RHU Phase II)

4.15 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit to the Bureau of the Treasury the unexpended balance totaling ₱14,334.38. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant record first the completed project in the Trust Fund books before transferring to the General Fund books of the Municipality and donating it to the Barangay, in compliance with item 6.5.7.2 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-008, as follows:

Account Name | Code | Debit | Credit
Road Networks | 1-07-03-010 | ₱5,493,216.88 | 
Government Equity | 3-01-01-010 |  | ₱5,493,216.88
To recognize completed infrastructure assets in the books of the Municipality. | To recognize completed infrastructure assets in the books of the Municipality. | To recognize completed infrastructure assets in the books of the Municipality. | To recognize completed infrastructure assets in the books of the Municipality.

5.9 	We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to determine the compositions of the unremitted withheld premiums of ₱962,812.66 for GSIS, ₱62,849.52 for PHIC and ₱17,500.00 for Pag-ibig and immediately remit to the concerned agency, where applicable. In addition, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that the exact amount of future premiums/loan amortizations are withheld and promptly remitted to the respective agency.
6.2 	We have reiterated our recommendation that Management continue its efforts to collect unsettled disallowances.

Demand Letters were sent and continuous monitoring through home visitations were also conducted.
The accounting office coordinated with the agriculture office. However, the agriculture office do not have records or files of the members’/beneficiaries who availed of the livelihood assistance.
Guidelines drafted by the Agriculture Office and MSWDO were presented for approval and finalization to the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan.

Four of the created positions namely Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer II, Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Assistant, Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer I and Administrative Aide IV were properly filled up as of December 31, 2021.
Several notices for collection were sent to the concerned separated employee.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entry to correct the overstatement of the Food Supplies for Distribution account totaling ₱6,105,335.00; and henceforth, comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in the preparation of journal entries.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entry to correct the overstatement of the Other Personnel Benefit account totaling ₱514,500.00; and henceforth, comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit duly accomplished Report on the Physical Count of Inventories, maintain stock cards to record purchases and issuances of inventories and reconcile the same to the ledger card of the accounting office and update the same regularly; and
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock and reconcile the same with the stock cards of the treasury office to ensure fair presentation of inventory accounts in the financial statements and ensure that all purchases are supported with properly accomplished Requisition and Issue Slips and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to support charges to inventory expense accounts at any given period.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor comply with Section 22(c) of RA 7160 and Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the use of the lump-sum QRF appropriation.  Further, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that a Sanggunian Resolution to support the release and utilization of the fund is issued before using the fund.

5.8 	We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 journal entries; and
Require the submission of Office Order/s or any other similar document evidencing authority to report for work during the implementation of ECQ in the Municipality.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to determine savings from all implemented 20% Development Fund Projects including allotment for Engineering Overhead and recommend to the LCE for its reversion to un-appropriated surplus for re-appropriation to other priority projects such as the COVID-19 preventive measures pursuant to Section 322 of RA No. 7160;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to include in the succeeding annual/supplemental budget the reverted funds from savings from implemented projects; and
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the Local Risk Reduction and Management Office/Council to evaluate/re-assess the LGU’s current plans and programs if there is a need to include equitable allocation for COVID-19 preventive measure PPAs in the utilization of 20% DF pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No.1, s. 2020.
We have reiterated our recommendation that Management continue its efforts to collect unsettled disallowances.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:  henceforth, clearly provide qualitative and quantitative data that would support the gender issues identified in the GPB, especially if the PPA is intended to be a GAD-focused activity taking 100% of its allocation from GAD;  administer the HGDG test on the GAD PPAs in the preparation of GPB and AR, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget; and  if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation because of the lack of orientation, trainings and and seminars on how to go about the HGDG test. Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation because of the lack of orientation, trainings and and seminars on how to go about the HGDG test. Management did not conduct HGDG test on GAD PPAs for CY2019.
observations.reason: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation because of the lack of orientation, trainings and and seminars on how to go about the HGDG test. Management did not conduct HGDG test on GAD PPAs for CY2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management collect from the contractors concerned the under deducted liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Management did not implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management did not implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the
completed projects to their appropriate PPE account.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a
reconciliation of actual amounts in the SCBAA with that of the Statement
of Financial Performance using the format provided by COA Circular
No. 2016-004 and disclose these either on the face of the SCBAA or in the
notes to financial statements.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:

c. stop the use of petty cash fund to pay PS and MOOE expenses that
are not considered petty operating expenses, in compliance with COA
Circulars Nos. 97-002 and 2012-001; and

d. henceforth, designate a Disbursing Officer or the Municipal
Treasurer to draw cash advances for Personnel Services
expenditures.

We have recommended that Management:

d. thru the treasury office in coordination with the agriculture and social
welfare and development offices, collect the outstanding balances of
the receivables totaling ₱368,120.26;

e. direct the accounting office to coordinate with the agriculture and
social welfare and development offices for the individual names of
beneficiaries and maintain a subsidiary ledger for each
member/beneficiary in order that future payments can be readily
identified and properly posted; and

f. formulate guidelines on the implementation of livelihood programs of
the LGU particularly those involving the provision of capital and
ensure that memoranda of agreement are crafted before releasing any
funds to intended beneficiaries.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the office
collectors to remit daily their collections to the liquidating officer, and the
liquidating officer to deposit completely all collections to the authorized
depository bank, as required under Sections 8 and 26 of COA Circular
92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take- up the proposed adjusting journal entries (PAJEs) in Annex A, to adjust the understated PPE totaling ₱3,245,295.17; and, henceforth, recognize assets, specifically PPEs, using the criteria in Paragraphs 14 and 21 of PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to take-up the proposed adjusting journal entries (PAJEs) in Annex A, to adjust the understated PPE totaling the ₱3,245,295.17; and, henceforth, recognize assets, specifically PPEs, using the criteria in Paragraphs 14 and 21 of PPSAS 17.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries were made to take up the proposed adjusting entries thru JEV No. 101-2019-08-056d and 101-2019-08-063d; and assets were recognized in accordance with Paragraph 14 and 21 of PPSAS 17.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer-Designate, and Municipal Engineer to comply with the duties and responsibilities enumerated in the COA Circular No. 2015-008.

An adjustment will be made after receipt of the actual inventory from the GSO and Engineering Office

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) a request for the creation of the LDRRMO including the three (3) other mandatory staff positions, and the SB to expedite the enactment of said ordinance as required in Section 6, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121. Thereafter, the Local Chief Executive to hire permanent personnel for the created positions for the proper implementation of the Local Disaster.
observations.managementAction: The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office was created by virtue of Municipal Ordinance No. 93 Series of 2019 dated June 11, 2019. The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer II was created through Municipal Ordinance No. 51 Series of 2016 dated September 20, 2016.
observations.reason: The other mandatory positions were requested for creation in CY2020 which will be funded in CY2021. Not Implemented. In CY2019, the position of LDRRMO II is not yet filled up.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  require the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories under all funds of the Municipality, pursuant to Sections 103 and 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1, and to classify Inventories as required in PPSAS No. 12 and COA Circular No. 2015-009; and  require the requisitioning officer, the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to observe the general procedures, prepare and maintain the forms and reports for the receipt, requisitioning, and issuance of inventory as provided in the NGAS Manual, Volumes I and II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant implemented the audit recommendation. Perpetual inventory method was used in accounting inventories of the LGU. The audit recommendation was implemented.
observations.reason: Perpetual inventory method was used in accounting inventories of the LGU. The audit recommendation was implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management issue demand letters to identified persons liable including those separated from the LGU service and continue efforts to collect unsettled disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management issued demand letters to identified persons liable including those who are separated from government service.
observations.reason: Management submitted copies of demand letters sent to liable persons on July 10, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that Management strategize and strengthen its efforts for the collection of these long outstanding receivables from the NGOs/POs and individuals.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to collect from various individuals the amount of P17,100.00 for CY2019.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management immediately cause the settlement of the long outstanding audit disallowances of former employees either by collecting directly from them, or by collecting from the persons solidarily liable.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation. Disallowances was greatly reduced from ₱367,535.97 to ₱33,305.58.
observations.reason: Disallowances was greatly reduced from ₱367,535.97 to ₱33,305.58.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management:  consider the quantity and nature of development projects being proposed for inclusion in the budget, so that these are proportionate to the capacity of available resources in the implementation of projects, especially the manpower resources of the Municipal Engineering Office; and  strategize with the Municipal Engineering Office and other offices concerned to fast track the implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: To date, management accomplished 93.36% of the said unimplemented projects. The remaining 6.64% of the 9 projects were still for implementation. Four of which were for solid waste management programs where implementation site are still under negotiation. The rest have various issues that are still to be settled. Management implemented the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The remaining 6.64% of the 9 projects were still for implementation. Four of which were for solid waste management programs where implementation site are still under negotiation. The rest have various issues that are still to be settled.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management:  through the Municipal Assessor, clean-up the General Revision Assessment Roll to establish a realistic and accurate RPT and SET receivables; and appraised, assessed and verified existing tax declarations is being updated every year through Supplemental Assessment Rolls which are being furnished to the Treasury Office. The Annual Report of Real Property Assessments which contain the total basic and SEF taxes to be collected is also furnished to the Treasury Office.  through the Municipal Treasurer, update her records as to RPT/SET Receivable records basing on the updated Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2014 General Revision containing updated Market Values and Assessed Values of appraised, assessed and verified existing tax declarations is being updated every year through Supplemental Assessment Rolls which are being furnished to the Treasury Office. The Annual Report of Real Property Assessments which contain the total basic and SEF taxes to be collected is also furnished to the Treasury Office. The Municipal Treasurer updated her records basing on the revised Assessment Roll.
observations.reason: The CY 2014 General Revision containing updated Market Values and Assessed Values of appraised, assessed and verified existing tax declarations is being updated every year through Supplemental Assessment Rolls which are being furnished to the Treasury Office. The Annual Report of Real Property Assessments which contain the total basic and SEF taxes to be collected is also furnished to the Treasury Office. The Municipal Treasurer updated her records basing on the revised Assessment Roll.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that:  through the Municipal Assessor’s Office, complete tax mapping operations by providing them adequate funds, modern equipment, manpower and other needed support, for them to come up with reliable RPT records that will establish a permanent link between the real properties in the field and the property assessment and tax records in the office; and  thereafter, ensure that reports submitted to the Municipal Treasurer, which are almost forwarded to the Municipal Accountant, are accurate and reliable so that the tax burden will be distributed equitably, and proper accounting thereof be established.
observations.managementAction: Tax mapping equipment of the LGU was not completed. The office lobbied for the procurement of a new surveying equipment which was delivered to the LGU only in February 2020. Management ensured the submission of accurate reports.
observations.reason: Tax mapping equipment of the LGU was not completed. The office lobbied for the procurement of a new surveying equipment which was delivered to the LGU only in February 2020. Management ensured the submission of accurate reports.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require all elected officials and heads of offices to undergo capability-building programs on GAD. For economy, invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize the participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices, employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay officials, to harmonize GAD-related activities in the ensuing year. Such capability building programs on GAD shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and others.
observations.managementAction: Management did not conduct capability-building programs on GAD because of non-availability of resource speakers.
observations.reason: Management did not conduct capability-building programs on GAD because of non-availability of resource speakers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to:  follow-up the approval of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan from the NSWMC, and thereafter, request for funding of relevant projects from the National SWM Fund in accordance with their guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan was approved October 29, 2018. Construction of MFR was requested and funded by DENR-EMB CAR. Other solid waste programs/projects were funded by the LGU.
observations.reason: The 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan was approved October 29, 2018. Construction of MFR was requested and funded by DENR-EMB CAR. Other solid waste programs/projects were funded by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management, henceforth, coordinate with the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the SEF to meet the existing and growing demands of basic education services, of which the Fund intends to provide. Further, to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly update the LSB of availability of funds for appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation. Out of the total allocation of ₱177,100.00, the LGU was able to utilize ₱175,100.00 or 99% of its SEF budget for CY2019.
observations.reason: Out of the total allocation of ₱177,100.00, the LGU was able to utilize ₱175,100.00 or 99% of its SEF budget for CY2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Initial steps were undertaken by the LGU for the disposal of said property. Management submitted to the audit team in February 2020 a request for inspection and appraisal of said property.

observations.reason: Initial steps were undertaken by the LGU for the disposal. Management submitted to the audit team in February 2020 a request for inspection and appraisal of said property from the Commission on Audit.

observations.managementAction: Management has deducted from salaries of officials and employees, leaving a balance of only P3,440.00, due from an employee who is no longer in government service.

observations.recommendation: 1.13	We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take-up the proposed adjusting journal entries (PAJEs) in Annex A, to adjust the understated PPE totaling P3,245,295.17; and, henceforth, recognize assets, specifically PPEs, using the criteria in Paragraphs 14 and 21 of PPSAS 17.

3.9	We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) a request for the creation of the LDRRMO including the three (3) other mandatory staff positions, and the SB to expedite the enactment of said ordinance as required in Section 6, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121.  Thereafter, the Local Chief Executive to hire permanent personnel for the created positions for the proper implementation of the Local Disaster.

require the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories under all funds of the Municipality, pursuant to Sections 103 and 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1, and to classify Inventories as required in PPSAS No. 12 and COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
require the requisitioning officer, the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to observe the general procedures, prepare and maintain the forms and reports for the receipt, requisitioning, and issuance of inventory as provided in the NGAS Manual, Volumes I and II.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendation that Management strategize and strengthen
its efforts for the collection of these long outstanding receivables from the NGOs/POs
and individuals.

We have recommended that Management instruct the collectors to remit promptly
their collections to the Municipal Treasurer, and the latter to deposit promptly all
collections turned over to her in an Authorized Government Depository Bank to avoid
exposure to theft or misuse.

We have recommended that Management immediately cause the settlement of the
long outstanding disallowances of former employees either by collecting directly
from them, or by collecting from the persons solidarily liable.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
 henceforth, clearly provide qualitative and quantitative data that would
support the gender issues identified in the GPB, especially if the PPA is
intended to be a GAD-focused activity taking 100% of its allocation from
GAD;
 administer the HGDG test on the GAD PPAs in the preparation of GPB
and AR, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be
attributed to the GAD Budget; and
 if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the
incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of
and/administration of the HGDG.

We have recommended that Management:
 consider the quantity and nature of development projects being
proposed for inclusion in the budget, so that these are proportionate
to the capacity of available resources in the implementation of
projects, especially the manpower resources of the Municipal
Engineering Office;
 advise the Municipal Development Council through the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator to evaluate the technical
viability of proposed development projects before these are being
requested for appropriation to avoid realignments; and
 strategize with the Municipal Engineering Office and other offices
concerned to fast track the implementation of projects.

We have recommended and
Management agreed to:
 strictly adhere to laws, rules
and regulations in the granting,
utilization and liquidation of
cash advances as provided in
COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997;
 expedite the disbursement of
social pension funds to the
senior citizens;
 through the Municipal
Accountant, regularly monitor
the liquidation of cash
advances of officers and
employees, and to deduct
outstanding cash advances
from salaries and other
monetary claims of such
officers and employees; and
 discontinue partial liquidation
of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
2.6      We have recommended and management agreed to:
a) strategize and strengthen its efforts for the collection of these long outstanding receivables from the NGOs/POs and individuals;
b)  identify and validate each item in the account to determine collectibility, and may request for authority to write-off the accountabilities of deceased payees in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
c) henceforth, cover all pecuniary agreements between LGU and other NGOs/POs/individuals with MOA, for the protection of the LGU.

4.9	We have recommended and management agreed that:
a.  through the Municipal Assessor’s Office, complete tax mapping operations by providing them adequate funds, modern equipment, manpower and other needed support, for them to come up with reliable RPT records that will establish a permanent link between the real properties in the field and the property assessment and tax records in the office;
b.	alongside, effectively educate constituents on the merits of a rational and responsive real property assessment and appraisal system through tax mapping; and
c.   thereafter, ensure that reports submitted to the Municipal Treasurer, which are forwarded to the Municipal Accountant, are accurate and reliable so that the tax burden will be distributed equitably, and proper accounting thereof be established.

12.6	We have recommended and management agreed to require all elected officials and heads of offices to undergo capability-building programs on GAD.  For economy, invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize the participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices, employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay officials, to harmonize GAD-related activities in the ensuing year. Such capacity building programs on GAD shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and others.

15.7	We reiterate our recommendations that management:
henceforth, appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund in conformity with the priority programs and projects set forth under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No 2011-1; and
revert the unutilized funds of the unimplemented ineligible projects for re-appropriation to projects identified under the above Joint Memorandum Circular.

16.8	We have recommended and management agreed to:
a.  consider the quantity and nature of development projects being proposed for inclusion in the budget, so that these are proportionate to the capacity of available resources in the implementation of projects, especially the manpower resources of the Municipal Engineering Office;
b.  through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, evaluate the technical viability of proposed development projects before these are being requested for appropriation to avoid realignments; and
c. strategize with the Municipal Engineering Office and other offices concerned to fast track the implementation of projects.
